Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: TurboQuant-WASM – Google's vector quantization in the browser 142 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. Ruckus: Racket for iOS 81 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. OpenScreen is an open-source alternative to Screen Studio 106 upvotes, 15 comments
  4. Show HN: M. C. Escher spiral in WebGL inspired by 3Blue1Brown 22 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Show HN: I made open source, zero power PCB hackathon badges 55 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. Show HN: A game where you build a GPU 520 upvotes, 138 comments
  7. AWS Engineer Reports PostgreSQL Perf Halved by Linux 7.0, Fix May Not Be Easy 110 upvotes, 23 comments
  8. The Indie Internet Index – submit your favorite sites 105 upvotes, 22 comments
  9. Training mRNA Language Models Across 25 Species for $165 125 upvotes, 28 comments
  10. Embarrassingly simple self-distillation improves code generation 555 upvotes, 167 comments
  11. Some Unusual Trees 255 upvotes, 74 comments
  12. Plague Ships (2020) 44 upvotes, 8 comments
  13. LLM Wiki – example of an "idea file" 79 upvotes, 20 comments
  14. Introduction to Computer Music [pdf] 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Breaking Enigma with Index of Coincidence on a Commodore 64 24 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. IBM 3270 Information Display System: Color and Programmed Symbols (1979) [pdf] 42 upvotes, 10 comments
  17. The Cathedral, the Bazaar, and the Winchester Mystery House 169 upvotes, 60 comments
  18. Components of a Coding Agent 175 upvotes, 63 comments
  19. Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs 371 upvotes, 162 comments
  20. Advice to Young People, the Lies I Tell Myself (2024) 27 upvotes, 7 comments
  21. How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'? 432 upvotes, 220 comments
  22. A case study in testing with 100+ Claude agents in parallel 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens 128 upvotes, 65 comments
  24. Show HN: Contrapunk – Real-time counterpoint harmony from guitar input, in Rust 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. The CMS is dead, long live the CMS 122 upvotes, 76 comments
  26. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 1037 upvotes, 786 comments
  27. Isseven 20 upvotes, 11 comments
  28. Functional programming accellerates agentic feature development 9 upvotes, 3 comments
  29. Shooting down ideas is not a skill 88 upvotes, 84 comments
  30. Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model 143 upvotes, 158 comments
